sect1.title: Introduction
sect1.para: The dataloader module is responsible for managing the graph data, including loading, caching, batching, and splitting it into training, validation, and test sets.

sect1.title: The dataloader Class
sect1.para: The dataloader class is the main component of this module.

listitem.para: dataloader(): The constructor initializes the data structures for storing the graphs and their indices.
~dataloader(): The destructor cleans up all the allocated memory, including the graph objects, data maps, and k-fold sets.
void extract_data(graph_t* gr): Adds a graph_t object to the dataset. It also performs a cleaning step to avoid duplicating the data maps.
void datatransfer(torch::TensorOptions* op, size_t* num_ev, size_t* cur_evnt): Transfers the graph data to a specific device (e.g., a GPU).
void datatransfer(std::map<int, torch::TensorOptions*>* ops): Transfers the graph data to multiple devices in parallel.

std::vector<graph_t*>* build_batch(std::vector<graph_t*>* data, model_template* mdl, model_report* rep): This is the core method for creating batches of graphs. It takes a vector of graph_t objects and a model template, and returns a vector of batched graphs. The batching process involves concatenating the node and edge features of the individual graphs into larger tensors.
void generate_kfold_set(int k): Splits the training set into k folds for cross-validation.
void generate_test_set(float percentage): Splits the entire dataset into a training set and a test set.
std::vector<graph_t*>* get_k_train_set(int k): Returns the training set for a specific k-fold.
std::vector<graph_t*>* get_k_validation_set(int k): Returns the validation set for a specific k-fold.
std::vector<graph_t*>* get_test_set(): Returns the test set.
std::map<std::string, std::vector<graph_t*>>* get_inference(): Returns the dataset organized by filename for inference.
void dump_dataset(std::string path): Saves the k-fold information to a file.
bool restore_dataset(std::string path): Restores the k-fold information from a file.
bool dump_graphs(std::string path, int threads): Serializes the graph objects and saves them to HDF5 files for caching. This is a multi-threaded process.
void restore_graphs(std::vector<std::string> path, int threads): Restores the graph objects from HDF5 cache files.
void restore_graphs(std::string path, int threads): A convenience method to restore graphs from a directory of HDF5 files.
void cuda_memory_server(): A server that runs in a separate thread to monitor and manage CUDA memory usage. It offloads graph data from the GPU to the CPU when the GPU memory is close to full.
void start_cuda_server(): Starts the CUDA memory server.
